ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned repeatedly across multiple threads (r/Cinema) as the quintessential film with a devastatingly depressing ending. Universally cited as a powerful but difficult watch that leaves a lasting impact.

ranked_movies.title: The Mist
ranked_movies.reason: Topped the list in the 'most depressing ending' thread. Its bleak and shocking conclusion is a major point of discussion and is frequently referenced as a benchmark for tragic endings.

ranked_movies.title: Grave of the Fireflies
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently brought up as one of the most emotionally devastating and depressing films ever made. Its portrayal of the consequences of war on children is a central point of discussion.

ranked_movies.title: Manchester by the Sea
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended in threads requesting depressing and introspective movies. Praised for its raw and realistic portrayal of grief and depression.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted in discussions about films that get progressively more depressing and messed up. The Korean original is specifically noted for its twisted narrative and bleak themes.

ranked_movies.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
ranked_movies.reason: Listed by OOP as a favorite introspective and depressing film. It's a widely discussed and recommended movie for its unique take on heartbreak, memory, and love.

ranked_movies.title: Melancholia
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times for its accurate and powerful depiction of depression and anxiety. Discussed as a masterpiece of mood and atmosphere that resonates deeply with those who experience mental health struggles.

ranked_movies.title: Atonement
ranked_movies.reason: Highly upvoted comment in the depressing ending thread described its ending as genuinely devastating and something that 'fucked them up,' making it a standout recommendation.

ranked_movies.title: The Road
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in both the depressing ending and progressively messed-up movie threads. Its post-apocalyptic bleakness and ambiguous ending are key points of discussion.

ranked_movies.title: Leaving Las Vegas
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in threads for depressing and introspective films. Known for its unflinching portrayal of self-destruction and alcoholism.

ranked_movies.title: Pan's Labyrinth
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed for its brutally depressing ending set against the backdrop of the Spanish Civil War. Noted for blending fantasy with harsh reality.

ranked_movies.title: Bridge to Terabithia
ranked_movies.reason: A classic example cited in the depressing ending thread for shocking viewers with its tragic turn, especially for a film perceived as a children's fantasy.

ranked_movies.title: All Of Us Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically mentioned in the gay movies thread as having an ending that was 'a punch in the gut,' highlighting its emotional impact within queer cinema.

ranked_movies.title: First Reformed
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a top-tier film for deep, introspective thoughts about existential despair and depression.

ranked_movies.title: Thunderbolts*
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of extensive discussion in r/Marvel and r/movies. While a newer release, it was passionately analyzed for its themes of trauma, depression, and functional mental illness, making it a frequently discussed film in this dataset.

ranked_movies.title: Bird Cage
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in the gay movies thread as a positive and fun counterpoint to typically tragic queer films. Celebrated for its humor and heart.

ranked_movies.title: Lost in Translation
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a favorite introspective film and recommended for its themes of loneliness and connection.

ranked_movies.title: The Machinist
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its dark, psychological themes and as a notable example of a depressing and introspective storyline.

ranked_movies.title: Synecdoche, New York
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a deeply introspective and complex film that deals with themes of art, life, and mental decay.

ranked_movies.title: Call Me By Your Name
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in the gay movies thread. While noted for its downbeat ending, it was praised for its message about valuing love and human connection, even when fleeting.
